content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers. Your task is to traverse the array using a pointer and decrement the pointer to access the previous elements of the array.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer `n`, representing the size of the array.

The second line contains `n` space-separated integers, representing the elements of the array.



Output Format
Print the elements of the array in reverse order, each element on a new line, by decrementing the pointer from the last element to the first.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 15), the size of the array. The second line contains n space‑separated integers arr[i] (1 ≤ arr[i] ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the n elements of the array in reverse order, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with n=5. The array is printed from the last element to the first.
Single element array; reversing does not change order.

solution.approach: We simply store the given array and then output its elements starting from the last index down to the first. The pointer‑decrement idea is simulated by iterating the index variable backwards. This requires O(n) time per test case and O(n) auxiliary space for the array. Since n ≤ 15, this is trivial for the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) per test case for the array storage

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int[] arr=new int[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++){arr[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());}for(int i=n-1;i>=0;i--){sb.append(arr[i]).append('\n');}}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
